title: Luscious Pillowy Portuguese Khus Khus Tagine
description: A nutty and comforting Portuguese-inspired dish made with khus khus, kielbasa, and creamy milk, perfect for a cold day.
instructions: Combine graham flour, water, and a pinch of salt in a bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a thin circle.
Heat a hot stone plate over medium heat and cook the dough circles for 2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
While the dough is cooking, heat the lardons in a pan over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the cooked dough circles from the stone plate and place them on a plate.
Sprinkle the khus khus, grated cheddar, and chopped parsley over the dough circles.
Drizzle the unsalted lard over the khus khus mixture.
Add the onion, garlic, kielbasa, and red pepper flakes to the pan with the lardons and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the khus khus mixture to the pan and stir to combine.
Pour in the whole milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 10 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Break the eggs into the sauce and stir to combine.
Serve the Luscious Pillowy Portuguese Khus Khus Tagine hot.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
